features.summary: Walmart reported strong Q1 FY2022 results with 43% adjusted EPS growth and broad-based sales momentum across all segments. US comp sales grew 6% with 37% eCommerce growth, while Sam's Club delivered 10.6% comp growth excluding fuel and tobacco. The company gained grocery market share and saw improving store traffic trends, with transactions turning positive in April for the first time in a year. International segment showed 5.1% constant currency growth despite recent divestitures, with strong performance in India, Canada, and China.
features.management_outlook: Management raised full-year guidance, now expecting low-to-mid single-digit consolidated sales growth and high single-digit operating income growth, up from previous flat-to-slight increase guidance. They anticipate Q2 EPS to be up low single-digits with continued grocery market share gains. The company is investing aggressively in pickup and delivery capacity to get ahead of demand and expects the second half to have more uncertainty than normal, though they maintain optimism about their competitive position and omnichannel strategy execution.
features.risks: The company faces ongoing COVID-19 challenges in international markets, particularly India, Canada, Chile and South Africa where resurgent cases are impacting operations. Supply chain pressures include port delays, transit challenges, and inflationary pressures in both cost of goods and wages. Inventory tightness persists in select general merchandise categories like adult bicycles and consumer electronics. The guidance assumes no significant additional government stimulus packages and continued improvement in COVID conditions.
features.key_drivers: Alternative profit streams are gaining momentum with triple-digit growth in Walmart Connect advertising revenue, strong marketplace GMV, and expanding fulfillment services. The company is building capacity ahead of demand in pickup and delivery services while expanding its omnichannel capabilities. Strategic acquisitions like Zeekit for virtual fitting rooms and MeMD for telehealth services support growth in higher-margin categories. Government stimulus provided meaningful tailwinds in Q1, particularly benefiting general merchandise categories as customers increased spending on reopening-related items.
